quiz_title: AL — Introduction - Civilizational Development and Lifestyle [EN]
topic: The Response to Lifestyle Problems Caused by Civilizational Development

questions.text: What best characterises the impact of civilizational development on everyday physical activity?
questions.explanation: As a result of technological progress, a significant part of human labour became mechanised, leading to a decrease in everyday physical activity.

options.text: A continuous increase in physical activity
A significant decrease in physical activity
Physical activity appearing exclusively in sport

questions.text: Modern technological development increased everyday physical load.
questions.explanation: False, because technological development reduced the need for physical labour.

questions.text: What led to the emergence of a sedentary lifestyle?
questions.explanation: The spread of mechanisation and sedentary work reduced daily physical activity.

options.text: The spread of mechanisation and sedentary work

questions.text: A sedentary lifestyle can lead to health problems in the long term.
questions.explanation: True, because lack of physical activity increases the risk of chronic diseases.

questions.text: What problem does the fitness trend address?
questions.explanation: The fitness trend is a response to the negative effects of a sedentary lifestyle.

options.text: The consequences of a sedentary lifestyle

questions.text: Which statement best describes the role of recreation in modern society?
questions.explanation: Recreation means conscious movement that helps maintain health alongside a sedentary lifestyle.

questions.text: For the modern person, movement is no longer an automatic part of daily life.
questions.explanation: True, because a significant part of physical activity has dropped out of the daily routine.

questions.text: Why has conscious movement become necessary in the modern lifestyle?
questions.explanation: Because natural physical load is missing from everyday life.

options.text: Because there is more leisure time
Because it has been made compulsory
Because the significance of sport has decreased

questions.text: One consequence of civilizational development is the automatic replacement of physical activity.
questions.explanation: False, because movement is not replaced automatically — it must be consciously incorporated.

questions.text: Which consequence is NOT characteristic of a sedentary lifestyle?
questions.explanation: A sedentary lifestyle does not increase but rather decreases physical performance.

questions.text: What is one fundamental contradiction of the modern lifestyle?
questions.explanation: Modern life reduces physical activity while increasing the need for it.

options.text: Little movement, but a greater need for it

questions.text: The goal of recreational movement forms is to maximise performance.
questions.explanation: False, because the primary goal of recreation is health preservation and regeneration.

questions.text: Which factor contributed most to the decrease in physical activity?
questions.explanation: Technological development and mechanisation brought about the greatest change.

questions.text: What is one of the most important roles of the concept of fitness in modern society?
questions.explanation: Fitness helps to counterbalance the negative effects of a sedentary lifestyle.
